title: Use a narrow OOF stack: feature-heavy GBDT + ordered CatBoost + hazard model, then regime-wise monotone calibration
summary: Stacking helps when base learners have different biases, and ordered CatBoost is specifically designed to reduce prediction shift from categorical leakage.
rationale: Stacking helps when base learners have different biases, and ordered CatBoost is specifically designed to reduce prediction shift from categorical leakage. A small ensemble of genuinely different views of the problem is more promising than blending many similar tree models, especially if calibration is done strictly on OOF predictions.
implementation_hint: Train three branches only: current engineered tabular model, a lean CatBoost on raw categorical/time-order inputs with ordered boosting, and the multi-horizon hazard model. Build OOF predictions, then fit a simple second-level logistic or near-isotonic calibrator separately for dry vs wet and early/mid/late race regimes; skip branches whose OOF correlation is too high.
expected_effect: Incremental AUC lift from complementary inductive biases and better probability ordering within specific race regimes.
risk: Wet-regime sample sizes may be too small for separate calibrators, and any non-OOF stacking will overfit immediately.
